name: Buddy Guy
abstract: George "Buddy" Guy (born July 30, 1936) is an American blues guitarist and singer-songwriter. He is an exponent of Chicago blues who has influenced generations of guitarists including Eric Clapton, Jimi Hendrix, Jimmy Page, Keith Richards, Stevie Ray Vaughan, Jeff Beck, Gary Clark Jr., and John Mayer.

concerts.label: Lead Belly Fest
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Junior Mance Part I
about_quotes.evidence: I did a record date once... with Buddy Guy and Junior Wells... It’s called “Buddy and the Juniors.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Junior Mance Part I interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Roscoe Mitchell
about_quotes.evidence: They were going to put together a band. But they weren't living in the house for another 30 days so they gave us the house. It was in Mill Valley. So we lived there... I believe it was and Buddy Guy — not Buddy Guy, God — Buddy Miles you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roscoe Mitchell interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 23, 2023
